chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 121 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.558/2023 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 08.02.2023 Mr. Shabbir Ali Bozdar, Advocate for the petitioners along with petitioners. Respondent No.5, present in person. Mr. Ghulam Abbas Kubar, Assistant Advocate General Sindh. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- S.I.P. Muhammad Iqbal Rajput, Investigating Officer of Police Station ‘A’ Section, Ghotki is present and files copy of statement of petitioner No.1 recorded under Section 161, Cr.P.C. He states that as per her statement, petitioner No.1 has deposed that she, being sui juris, has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2 and she was not earlier married to any other person. Investigating Officer further states that he will conclude his investigation by making the statement of petitioner No.1 part of the final challan under Section 173, Cr.P.C. and submit such report before the Judicial Magistrate concerned for obtaining orders in accordance with law. Learned Counsel for the petitioner, on being satisfied with the statement of Investigating Officer, does not press instant petition further, however, states that the police may be directed to provide protection to the petitioners as they apprehend their murder by the hands of private respondent. C. P. No. D – 121 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 S.H.O, Police Station Ghotki is directed to provide necessary protection to the petitioners in accordance with law as and when required by them. At this juncture, learned Counsel for the petitioners states that the petitioners apprehend danger to their lives from private respondent as many people of private respondent are available outside of the Court; hence, he requests for providing the police escort to their destination.

D – 121 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 S.H.O, Police Station Ghotki is directed to provide necessary protection to the petitioners in accordance with law as and when required by them. At this juncture, learned Counsel for the petitioners states that the petitioners apprehend danger to their lives from private respondent as many people of private respondent are available outside of the Court; hence, he requests for providing the police escort to their destination. Learned D.P.G. is directed to arrange/provide the police escort to the petitioners to the extent of territorial limits of District Sukkur. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed application. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
